ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cheesecake class (FOODON_03301116); no specific lemon meringue cheesecake subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC represents cheesecake as a category. No specific lemon meringue cheesecake code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for lemon meringue cheesecake or cheesecake as a food product at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of lemon meringue cheesecake, approximately 120 g, as typically served at a café or coffee chain in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, lemon (USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy, NDB No. 18960 / Foundation Foods composite for lemon cheesecake)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for lemon cheesecake (NDB 18960) and plain cheesecake (NDB 18159), combined with USDA data for meringue topping and graham cracker crust components. The Starbucks Singapore serving is a commercial product; exact formulation is not publicly disclosed. Values represent a best-estimate composite for a lemon cheesecake with graham cracker crust and meringue topping, consistent with USDA SR Legacy category data. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake, plain — Glycemic Index Database; analogous estimate applied to lemon meringue cheesecake variant
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 40 (low) for plain cheesecake. Lemon meringue cheesecake shares a similar cream cheese and graham cracker base; the meringue topping adds refined sugar which may marginally elevate GI, but the high fat and protein content of the cream cheese base suppresses glycemic response. GI of 40 is applied as a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (40 × 35.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 16.8 g.

hypertension.guidance: Lemon meringue cheesecake contains approximately 220 mg sodium per 100 g (264 mg per 120 g serving), and individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and frequency of consumption as part of a sodium-controlled diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar and refined carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 24.5 g of sugar and 35 g of total carbohydrate per 100 g, lemon meringue cheesecake poses a significant glycemic burden for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions, with blood glucose monitoring advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lemon meringue cheesecake is high in saturated fat (10.2 g/100 g), contains trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), and provides approximately 95 mg cholesterol per 100 g, making it a food that individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume sparingly and infrequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Meringue Cheesecake is a Western-style dessert consisting of a creamy lemon-flavoured cream cheese filling set on a buttery graham cracker (digestive biscuit) crust, topped with a layer of toasted or baked meringue made from whipped egg whites and sugar. The filling is typically prepared by blending cream cheese, sugar, eggs, lemon juice, and lemon zest, then baking or chilling until set. The meringue topping is piped or spread over the surface and either torched or briefly baked to achieve a golden, lightly caramelised exterior. The flavour profile combines the rich, tangy creaminess of the cheesecake base with the bright citrus acidity of lemon and the airy sweetness of meringue. The texture contrasts a dense, smooth filling with a crisp crust and a soft, pillowy topping. In Singapore, this dessert is served at Western-style cafés and coffee chains such as Starbucks, where it is positioned as a premium café pastry. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (approximately 321 kcal per 100 g), high in saturated fat, sugar, and refined carbohydrates, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for vegans and contains common allergens including dairy, eggs, and gluten from the graham cracker crust.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of lemon meringue cheesecake?
Is lemon meringue cheesecake suitable for diabetics?
What allergens are in lemon meringue cheesecake?
Is lemon meringue cheesecake high in sugar?
What is the GI of cheesecake?
Can I eat cheesecake if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between lemon cheesecake and lemon meringue cheesecake?
Is Starbucks lemon meringue cheesecake halal?

confidence_reason: The Starbucks Singapore lemon meringue cheesecake is a commercial product whose exact nutritional formulation is not publicly disclosed. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for lemon cheesecake and component ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cheesecake. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for this food category. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA component data and are approximate. The halal status of the Starbucks Singapore product is not confirmed from an approved source and is set to false pending verification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 35.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 16.8 g for a 120 g slice.
